body:
Is there any feature added for package consumers? (Out of curiosity).
What's the dev reload time before/after?

@vvo 

I will come with real stats in the end, but it's already a big win to have a single webpack handling all the stuff instead of having two CLI tool running for the dev environment (webpack & node-sass) separately.

It might not gain speed in term of bundling, but the end experience of hot reload in the browser is completely worth it while working on styles.

Features for the end users:

* we will ship real ES6 modules so it will be easier for them to have tree-shaking
* webpack3 scope hoisting should speed up the load of the library itself in the browser
* lower the build size of CSS & JS (not sure, investigation at the end)
@vvo I did a little comparison of build time for staging, dropped from 12s to 6s 💪

> I did a little comparison of build time for staging, dropped from 12s to 6s

What's staging (vs dev and prod)? What's the time for the actual dev?

* dev is build of library itself + stories + documentation
* staging is a static build of the dev without documentation
* prod is browser and modules build of the library itself

Will add more metrics when I'll be done with upgrading the documentation tools 👍

I can't upgrade easily the docgen build, `ms-webpack` is explicitly requiring webpack v1 (https://github.com/10xjs/ms-webpack/blob/master/package.json#L21) which is altering the configuration on the fly and make it incompatible with webpack 3 😩

So, I'm removing upgrade of docgen build from the scope of this PR.

Deploy preview ready!

Built with commit b7e1d96d3f01486f565feac6fdbf9cb0a9a4cffb

https://deploy-preview-2218--algolia-instantsearch.netlify.com

> I can't upgrade easily the docgen build, ms-webpack is explicitly requiring webpack v1

See https://github.com/10xjs/ms-webpack/issues/6
If you want to push new features or updates to ms-webpack, do it and we'll get them merged.
If you want to ditch it, ditch it it's so small plugin (150 lines).
@vvo Thanks with `ms-webpack` alpha.1 which does not require a specific webpack version it works like a charm 🙌

> This will imply documentation and won't work "out of the box" for existing projects, but it's non breaking and will provide really good results when you are not importing widgets or connectors.

Seems fine, in another major version we could ditch the version where we allow for importing the whole instantsearch + instantsearch.widgets* ... So users (of webpack/browserify) would be forced to uniquely ask for each method they want to use. Which is fine when you think about it (react is does that, nobody complains)

* I've updated the ES6 build export strategy
* I've added a paragraph about tree-shaking into usage.md
* I've published a `v2.1.0-beta.1` version with tag beta on npm in order to test correctly

I'm going to test the beta with `etch-instantsearch` which is only using the connectors API and also for my next AngularJS v4 experiment.

This should almost be ready for merge if my tests are conclusive 👍

HI @iam4x,
how are you?

My name is Asaf, and I'm the author of [autodll-webpack-plugin](https://github.com/asfktz/autodll-webpack-plugin).

I'm here because I searched GitHub for projects which currently implement my plugin to learn from their use cases, and see if they encountered any issues with it.

I noticed that the Plugin's context is incorrect, it should point to the root of your project.

So instead of:

```js
new AutoDllPlugin({
    inject: true,
    context: __dirname,
    filename: '[name].[hash].js',
    entry: {vendor: Object.keys(require('../package.json').dependencies)},
})
```

It should be:

```js
new AutoDllPlugin({
    inject: true,
    context: path.join(__dirname, '..'),
    filename: '[name].[hash].js',
    entry: {vendor: Object.keys(require('../package.json').dependencies)},
})
```

Incorrect context will result in having the same modules in both the DLL bundle **and** the main bundle.

That silent error comes from webpack's own DllPlugin itself.
I plan to add warnings about it soon.

**Stats before the change:**

```
Time: 8901ms
                                Asset       Size  Chunks                    Chunk Names
       bundle.bcfcc724970cd0307929.js    5.96 MB       0  [emitted]  [big]  bundle
instantsearch.bcfcc724970cd0307929.js     4.1 MB       1  [emitted]  [big]  instantsearch
                           index.html  690 bytes          [emitted]
       vendor.df506d98aa0c53bd74ce.js    1.43 MB          [emitted]  [big]
Child html-webpack-plugin for "index.html":
         Asset   Size  Chunks  Chunk Names
    index.html  27 kB       0
```

Average Rebuild:  ~950ms


**Stats after the change:**

```
Time: 5516ms
                                Asset       Size  Chunks                    Chunk Names
       bundle.6873c9409851d5449e9d.js    3.96 MB       0  [emitted]  [big]  bundle
instantsearch.6873c9409851d5449e9d.js     2.1 MB       1  [emitted]  [big]  instantsearch
                           index.html  690 bytes          [emitted]
       vendor.df506d98aa0c53bd74ce.js    1.43 MB          [emitted]  [big]
Child html-webpack-plugin for "index.html":
         Asset   Size  Chunks  Chunk Names
    index.html  27 kB       0
```

Average Rebuild:  ~550ms

You can optimize it even more by adding the "dev-novel" module to the DLL entries too (It's currently in devDependencies)

Average Rebuild:  ~450ms

Also, please update to version 0.0.8 as it fixes a critical bug.

Tip: add `debug: true` to the Plugin's settings to see more clearly what the plugin is doing.

Please let me know if you need any further help.
Asaf.

Thanks @asfktz that's awesome. This PR looks almost complete. Only @iam4x should be able to say so, let's wait a bit (he's currently not available).

Once we have more news, this is a great chore AND feature

@vvo 👍
Let me know if you need anything.
To keep fix easy to deploy, I propose that we reopen this PR targeting `feat/2.1` instead of `develop` (after we agree on the comments and all :) ). Thanks :)
Thank's a lot for your advices and code review @asfktz 👍
Closing it in favor of #2240 which is on the right branch @bobylito
